steps: Adjust the seat so the handles align with the middle of your chest at nipple height.
Sit tall with your back flat against the pad and plant your feet firmly on the floor.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades to set a stable base.
Grip the handles and press them forward until your arms are nearly straight without locking out.
Squeeze your chest at the end range, then control the handles back until you feel a stretch across your pecs.
Repeat for reps while keeping your shoulders pinned back throughout.
cues: Exhale as you press, inhale as you return.
Think about pushing through your chest, not your triceps.
Use a controlled two-second lowering tempo.
Keep a full but pain-free range of motion.
mistakes: Letting the shoulders shrug up and forward during the press.
Setting the seat too high or too low, shifting tension off the chest.
Cutting the range short by not stretching on the return.
Slamming the weight stack at the bottom.

steps: Set both pulleys to roughly shoulder height and select a light to moderate weight.
Sit or stand in the center with a slight forward lean and grab a handle in each hand.
Start with arms out wide and elbows softly bent, feeling a stretch across your chest.
Keep the elbow angle fixed and sweep your hands together in front of your chest in an arc.
Squeeze your pecs hard at peak contraction with hands nearly touching.
Slowly reverse the motion until you feel a deep stretch, then repeat.
cues: Move only at the shoulder joint, keeping elbows locked in a fixed bend.
Emphasize the stretch at the bottom for maximum pec growth.
Exhale as hands come together, inhale as they open.
Lead with the elbows, not the hands.
mistakes: Turning the flye into a press by bending and extending the elbows.
Using momentum or jerking the handles together.
Going too heavy and losing the chest stretch.
Rounding the upper back instead of keeping the chest proud.

steps: Set the bench to a 30 degree incline and sit with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Kick the dumbbells up to shoulder level as you lie back, then plant your feet.
Retract your shoulder blades and hold the dumbbells just outside your upper chest.
Press the dumbbells up and slightly inward until your arms are nearly extended.
Lower under control until your elbows are just below chest level and you feel a stretch.
Press back up and repeat, keeping wrists stacked over elbows.
cues: Keep a 30 to 45 degree elbow flare relative to your torso.
Exhale as you press, inhale on the way down.
Drive up and slightly together to bias the upper chest.
Control the descent for a two to three second count.
mistakes: Setting the incline too high, turning it into a shoulder press.
Flaring elbows to 90 degrees and stressing the shoulders.
Bouncing the dumbbells or cutting the depth short.
Clanking the dumbbells together at the top and losing tension.

steps: Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell on each thigh, then lie back kicking them to shoulder level.
Plant your feet flat and retract your shoulder blades into the bench.
Position the dumbbells at chest level with wrists stacked over your elbows.
Press the dumbbells up until your arms are nearly straight, bringing them slightly together.
Lower under control until your elbows dip just below chest height for a full stretch.
Press back up and repeat for the desired reps.
cues: Keep your shoulder blades pinned and chest up the whole set.
Exhale on the press, inhale on the descent.
Aim for a smooth arc rather than a straight vertical path.
Feel the stretch in the pecs at the bottom.
mistakes: Letting the shoulders round forward off the bench.
Bouncing the weights or using leg drive to cheat.
Flaring elbows too wide and straining the shoulders.
Pressing with an incomplete range of motion.

steps: Lie on a flat bench with your eyes under the bar and feet planted firmly on the floor.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Unrack the bar and hold it over your upper chest with arms extended.
Lower the bar under control to your lower chest, keeping elbows tucked at about 45 degrees.
Touch the chest lightly, then drive the bar up and slightly back over your shoulders.
Lock out at the top and repeat, re-racking safely when finished.
cues: Maintain a slight arch and keep your upper back tight.
Inhale before lowering, exhale as you drive up.
Keep wrists straight and stacked over the elbows.
Drive your feet into the floor for leg drive.
mistakes: Bouncing the bar off the chest.
Flaring the elbows out to 90 degrees.
Lifting the hips off the bench to press the weight.
Letting the bar drift toward the neck when lowering.

steps: Set an adjustable bench to about a 30 degree incline under the bar.
Lie back with feet planted and grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Retract your shoulder blades and unrack the bar over your upper chest.
Lower the bar under control to the top of your chest, keeping elbows tucked around 45 degrees.
Press the bar up and slightly back until your arms are extended.
Repeat for reps, then re-rack the bar safely.
cues: Keep the bar path over the upper chest, not the shoulders.
Inhale on the way down, exhale as you press.
Keep the shoulder blades pinned for a stable base.
Control the eccentric to protect the shoulders.
mistakes: Setting the incline too steep and overloading the front delts.
Bouncing the bar off the chest.
Flaring the elbows too wide.
Lowering the bar to the neck instead of the upper chest.

steps: Attach weight with a dip belt and mount the parallel bars with arms locked out.
Lean your torso slightly forward to bias the chest and keep your core braced.
Bend your elbows and lower yourself under control until your upper arms are about parallel to the floor.
Feel a stretch across the lower chest at the bottom.
Press back up by driving through your palms until your arms are nearly straight.
Repeat for reps, keeping the forward lean throughout.
cues: Lean forward to emphasize the chest, stay upright to bias triceps.
Lower for two seconds and control the descent.
Keep shoulders down and away from your ears.
Inhale on the way down, exhale as you press up.
mistakes: Descending too deep and overstretching the shoulders.
Shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
Using a bouncy, uncontrolled tempo.
Adding too much weight and cutting the range short.

steps: Adjust the seat so the handles sit at about chest height with your back flat against the pad.
Sit tall, plant your feet, and grasp the handles with a slight bend in your elbows.
Begin with your arms open and feel a stretch across your chest.
Squeeze the handles together in front of your chest using your pecs.
Hold the peak contraction briefly with the pads nearly touching.
Return slowly to the open, stretched position and repeat.
cues: Drive through your elbows and forearms, not your hands.
Squeeze and pause at peak contraction.
Exhale as you close, inhale as you open.
Control the negative for a deep chest stretch.
mistakes: Using momentum to slam the pads together.
Letting the shoulders roll forward during the squeeze.
Setting the seat height wrong so tension leaves the chest.
Cutting the stretch short at the open position.

steps: Set both pulleys to a high position and select a moderate weight.
Grab a handle in each hand and step forward into a staggered stance with a slight forward lean.
Start with arms out to the sides, elbows softly bent, feeling a chest stretch.
Sweep your hands down and together in front of your hips in a wide arc.
Squeeze your pecs hard as your hands cross or meet at the bottom.
Slowly return to the stretched starting position and repeat.
cues: Keep a fixed soft bend in the elbows throughout.
Squeeze the chest at the point where hands meet.
Exhale as you bring hands together, inhale as they open.
Maintain the forward lean and braced core for stability.
mistakes: Bending and extending the elbows like a press.
Standing too upright and losing chest tension.
Using body swing to move the handles.
Going too heavy and shortening the range of motion.

steps: Place a weight plate or wear a weighted vest across your upper back.
Set your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width with your body in a straight line.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes to keep a rigid plank.
Lower your chest toward the floor, keeping elbows at about a 45 degree angle.
Descend until your chest nearly touches the ground.
Press back up until your arms are extended and repeat.
cues: Keep a straight line from head to heels the whole time.
Lower under control, exhale as you push up.
Tuck elbows to protect the shoulders.
Push the floor away and spread the shoulder blades at the top.
mistakes: Letting the hips sag or pike up.
Flaring the elbows straight out to the sides.
Doing partial reps that never reach the floor.
Dropping the head or craning the neck.

steps: Lie on a flat bench and hold a single dumbbell with both hands cupping the top end.
Extend your arms over your chest with a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core and keep your hips stable on the bench.
Lower the dumbbell in an arc back behind your head until you feel a deep stretch.
Pull the dumbbell back over your chest using your lats and chest.
Stop above your chest and repeat for reps.
cues: Keep the elbow bend fixed throughout the movement.
Focus on the stretch behind the head for the lats.
Inhale as you lower, exhale as you pull over.
Keep the rib cage down and core tight to avoid arching.
mistakes: Bending and straightening the elbows excessively.
Overarching the lower back at the bottom.
Lowering too far and straining the shoulders.
Using momentum instead of a controlled arc.

steps: Lie on a flat bench holding two dumbbells or a plate pressed firmly together over your chest.
Squeeze the weights together as hard as you can throughout the set.
Position your hands centered over your mid-chest with palms facing each other.
Lower the weight under control to your chest, keeping the inward squeeze constant.
Press the weight straight back up while maintaining maximal pressure.
Lock out at the top and repeat, keeping the squeeze the entire time.
cues: Press the weights together hard to maximize inner-chest tension.
Keep the movement slow and deliberate.
Exhale as you press up, inhale as you lower.
Keep your shoulder blades retracted and chest up.
mistakes: Letting the weights drift apart and losing the squeeze.
Flaring the elbows out too wide.
Rushing the reps and losing control.
Rounding the shoulders forward off the bench.

steps: Lie on a flat bench and grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Retract your shoulder blades and unrack the bar over your upper chest.
Flare your elbows out and lower the bar toward your neck and clavicle line.
Descend under control until the bar is just above your upper chest, feeling a strong pec stretch.
Press the bar straight back up to the starting position.
Repeat with light, controlled weight and re-rack safely.
cues: Use lighter weight than a standard bench for safety.
Keep the movement slow and fully controlled.
Emphasize the deep upper-chest stretch.
Exhale as you press, inhale as you lower.
mistakes: Loading too heavy on this vulnerable shoulder position.
Lowering the bar too fast toward the neck.
Losing shoulder blade retraction.
Bouncing the bar at the bottom.

steps: Adjust the seat so the handles sit at about shoulder or ear height.
Sit tall with your back flat against the pad and feet planted.
Grip the handles with wrists stacked over your elbows.
Press the handles overhead until your arms are nearly straight without locking hard.
Lower under control until your elbows reach about shoulder level.
Repeat for reps, keeping your torso stable.
cues: Keep your core braced and avoid arching the lower back.
Exhale as you press up, inhale as you lower.
Keep shoulders down away from your ears.
Control the descent for a full stretch on the delts.
mistakes: Setting the seat too low and straining the shoulders.
Arching the back to push the weight.
Shrugging the traps up during the press.
Cutting the range short at the bottom.

steps: Set an upright bench and sit with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Kick the dumbbells up to shoulder height with palms facing forward.
Sit tall, brace your core, and keep your back against the pad.
Press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are nearly extended.
Lower under control until your elbows reach about ear level.
Repeat for reps, keeping the dumbbells stacked over your shoulders.
cues: Keep your core tight to avoid overarching.
Exhale as you press, inhale on the way down.
Keep wrists stacked over elbows throughout.
Control the lowering phase for a full deltoid stretch.
mistakes: Arching the lower back excessively.
Flaring the elbows too far forward or back.
Using momentum or leg drive to press.
Clanking the dumbbells together overhead.

steps: Set the barbell at collarbone height in a rack and grip it just outside shoulder-width.
Unrack the bar onto your front delts with elbows slightly in front of the bar.
Step back, set your feet shoulder-width, and brace your core and glutes.
Press the bar straight overhead, moving your head slightly back to clear a path.
Lock out with the bar over your mid-foot and shrug the shoulders up.
Lower under control back to your collarbone and repeat.
cues: Squeeze your glutes and brace hard to protect your spine.
Move your head back then through as the bar passes your face.
Exhale at the top, inhale before the next rep.
Keep the bar path vertical over your mid-foot.
mistakes: Excessive lower back arch or leaning back to press.
Pressing the bar around your face instead of moving your head.
Using leg drive that turns it into a push press.
Letting the elbows drift too far behind the bar.

steps: Set an adjustable bench to about a 30 degree incline inside the Smith machine.
Position the bench so the bar path lines up over your upper chest.
Lie back, retract your shoulder blades, and unrack the bar by rotating it out.
Lower the bar under control to your upper chest with elbows at about 45 degrees.
Press the bar up along the fixed track until your arms are nearly straight.
Repeat for reps, then rotate the bar to re-rack.
cues: Position the bench so the bar touches the upper chest.
Exhale as you press, inhale on the descent.
Keep shoulder blades pinned for a stable base.
Control the eccentric and avoid bouncing.
mistakes: Placing the bench so the bar hits the neck or lower chest.
Setting too steep an incline and overloading the delts.
Bouncing the bar off the chest.
Flaring elbows out to 90 degrees.

steps: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs.
Brace your core and keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Raise one or both dumbbells forward and up to about shoulder height.
Keep your palms facing down and wrists neutral as you lift.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower under control.
Repeat for reps without swinging your torso.
cues: Lift to shoulder height, no higher, to keep tension on the front delts.
Exhale as you raise, inhale as you lower.
Keep a slight elbow bend fixed throughout.
Move slowly and avoid using momentum.
mistakes: Swinging the torso to heave the weight up.
Raising the dumbbells too high past shoulder level.
Shrugging the traps during the lift.
Using too heavy a weight and losing control.

steps: Set a single pulley to the lowest position and stand side-on to the machine.
Grab the handle with the outside hand across your body and hold the frame for support.
Keep a slight bend in your elbow and let the working arm start across your body.
Raise the handle out to the side up to about shoulder height, leading with your elbow.
Pause at the top with your pinky slightly higher than your thumb.
Lower under control back across your body and repeat.
cues: Lead with the elbow, not the hand.
Keep the tension on the side delt through a long range.
Exhale as you raise, inhale as you lower.
Slightly tilt the pinky up at the top for extra delt engagement.
mistakes: Using body sway to swing the weight up.
Shrugging the trap instead of raising with the delt.
Going too heavy and shortening the range.
Raising the arm too far forward instead of out to the side.

steps: Set two low pulleys and grab the opposite handle in each hand so the cables cross.
Stand tall with a slight forward lean and arms down in front of you.
Keep a soft bend in your elbows and brace your core.
Raise both arms up and out in a Y shape overhead, thumbs leading.
Pause at the top with your arms forming a wide Y above your head.
Lower under control back to the crossed starting position and repeat.
cues: Form a clear Y overhead to hit the delts and lower traps.
Lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Exhale as you raise, inhale as you lower.
Keep the movement smooth and controlled.
mistakes: Using momentum to fling the arms up.
Shrugging heavily instead of raising through the delts.
Bending the elbows and turning it into a pull.
Overloading and losing the Y path.

steps: Attach a cuff or handle to a low pulley and stand side-on with the cable behind your back.
Grip the handle with the outside hand so the cable runs behind your body.
Keep a slight elbow bend and brace your core, standing tall.
Raise your arm out to the side up to shoulder height, leading with the elbow.
Pause at the top with the delt fully contracted.
Lower under control and repeat, keeping the cable behind you.
cues: Running the cable behind the back keeps constant tension on the side delt.
Lead with the elbow and keep the wrist neutral.
Exhale as you raise, inhale as you lower.
Move slowly through a full range of motion.
mistakes: Swinging or using body momentum.
Shrugging the trap up during the raise.
Letting the arm drift forward instead of out to the side.
Using too much weight and cutting the range short.

steps: Hold a dumbbell in one hand and grip a rack or pole with the other for support.
Lean your torso away from the working arm so it hangs across your body.
Keep a slight bend in the elbow and brace your core.
Raise the dumbbell out to the side up to shoulder height, leading with the elbow.
Pause briefly at the top with the side delt contracted.
Lower slowly back across the body and repeat, then switch sides.
cues: Leaning away lengthens the range and keeps tension at the bottom.
Lead with the elbow, not the hand.
Exhale as you raise, inhale as you lower.
Keep the tempo slow and controlled.
mistakes: Using momentum or bouncing at the bottom.
Shrugging the trap instead of using the delt.
Raising the arm forward rather than out to the side.
Going too heavy and losing control of the lean.

steps: Adjust the machine pads so they sit against your outer upper arms at your sides.
Stand tall inside the machine with your core braced and feet planted.
Position your elbows against the pads with a slight bend.
Drive your upper arms out and up to about shoulder height using the side delts.
Pause briefly at the top of the movement.
Lower under control back to your sides and repeat.
cues: Push through the elbows against the pads, not with the hands.
Raise to shoulder height for peak side-delt contraction.
Exhale as you raise, inhale as you lower.
Keep the traps relaxed and avoid shrugging.
mistakes: Using body momentum to bounce the weight up.
Shrugging the shoulders during the raise.
Setting the pads too high or too low on the arms.
Cutting the range of motion short.

steps: Adjust the seat so your shoulders align with the machine's pivot point.
Sit tall with your back against the pad and place your arms against the pads.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and brace your core.
Raise your upper arms out to the sides up to about shoulder height.
Pause at the top with the side delts fully contracted.
Lower under control back to the start and repeat.
cues: Sitting removes momentum, so let the delts do the work.
Drive through the elbows against the pads.
Exhale as you raise, inhale as you lower.
Avoid shrugging the traps up.
mistakes: Aligning the pivot wrong so the shoulder joint is off-axis.
Shrugging instead of raising with the delts.
Bouncing the weight at the bottom.
Stopping short of shoulder height.

steps: Stand tall holding a light dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing back.
Brace your core and keep your elbows nearly straight.
Raise the dumbbells out and up in a wide arc all the way overhead.
Rotate your palms as you lift so your thumbs point up near the top.
Touch or nearly touch the dumbbells overhead with arms extended.
Lower slowly along the same arc back to your sides and repeat.
cues: Use very light weight since this hits delts through a huge range.
Keep the arms long and sweep in a wide arc.
Exhale as you raise overhead, inhale as you lower.
Move slowly and control the full path.
mistakes: Going too heavy and using momentum.
Bending the elbows to cheat the weight up.
Rushing the reps and losing control.
Shrugging the traps instead of using the delts.

steps: Stand tall holding a barbell or dumbbells in front of your thighs with an overhand grip.
Set your hands about shoulder-width apart and brace your core.
Pull the weight straight up along your body, leading with your elbows.
Raise until your upper arms are about parallel to the floor at chest height.
Pause briefly with elbows high and above your wrists.
Lower under control back to the start and repeat.
cues: Lead with the elbows and keep the bar close to your body.
Stop at chest height to protect the shoulders.
Exhale as you pull, inhale as you lower.
Use a slightly wider grip to reduce shoulder strain.
mistakes: Pulling too high and impinging the shoulders.
Using a grip that is too narrow.
Swinging the torso to heave the weight.
Letting the wrists rise above the elbows.

steps: Set both pulleys to about shoulder height and grab the opposite handle in each hand so cables cross.
Stand tall with arms extended in front of you at shoulder height.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and brace your core.
Sweep your arms out and back in a wide arc, squeezing the rear delts.
Pull until your arms are out to your sides and shoulder blades retract.
Return under control to the crossed start and repeat.
cues: Lead with the pinkies and squeeze the rear delts at the back.
Keep the elbow bend fixed throughout.
Exhale as you pull apart, inhale as you return.
Focus on the rear delts, not the mid-back.
mistakes: Bending the elbows and turning it into a row.
Using momentum to fling the arms back.
Shrugging the traps instead of using rear delts.
Going too heavy and shortening the range.

steps: Set the pec deck handles to the forward position and sit facing the pad.
Adjust the seat so the handles are at about shoulder height.
Grip the handles with arms extended in front, elbows slightly bent.
Sweep your arms out and back in a wide arc, squeezing your rear delts.
Pull until your arms are in line with your body and shoulder blades gently retract.
Return under control to the start and repeat.
cues: Lead with the elbows and think about opening the chest.
Squeeze the rear delts at the end range.
Exhale as you pull back, inhale as you return.
Keep a fixed slight elbow bend.
mistakes: Overusing the mid-back and traps instead of rear delts.
Jerking the handles with momentum.
Setting the seat too high or too low.
Bending and extending the elbows like a row.

steps: Attach a rope to a pulley set at about face or upper-chest height.
Grab both rope ends with a neutral grip and step back to create tension.
Stand tall with a slight lean back and arms extended in front.
Pull the rope toward your face, flaring your elbows out and up.
Separate the rope ends and squeeze your rear delts with hands beside your ears.
Return under control to the extended start and repeat.
cues: Pull toward your forehead and think elbows high and wide.
Externally rotate so your knuckles point back at the top.
Exhale as you pull, inhale as you return.
Squeeze the rear delts and mid-back at peak.
mistakes: Pulling too low toward the chest instead of the face.
Using too much weight and leaning back to yank it.
Failing to externally rotate the shoulders.
Shrugging the traps instead of using rear delts.

steps: Hold a dumbbell in each hand and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is near parallel to the floor.
Let the dumbbells hang beneath your chest with palms facing each other.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and a flat back.
Raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, leading with your elbows.
Pause at the top with your rear delts squeezed and arms in line with your body.
Lower under control and repeat, keeping your torso still.
cues: Lead with the elbows and squeeze the rear delts.
Keep the elbow bend fixed throughout.
Exhale as you raise, inhale as you lower.
Keep the neck neutral and back flat.
mistakes: Standing too upright and shifting work to the traps.
Using momentum to swing the weights up.
Rounding the lower back at the hinge.
Raising with straight arms and stressing the elbows.

steps: Set an incline bench and lie chest-down with your torso supported on the pad.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand hanging below your shoulders with palms facing each other.
Keep a slight bend in the elbows and your head neutral.
Raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, leading with the elbows.
Squeeze the rear delts at the top with arms in line with your body.
Lower under control and repeat.
cues: Chest support removes momentum, so isolate the rear delts.
Lead with the elbows and squeeze at the top.
Exhale as you raise, inhale as you lower.
Keep a fixed slight bend in the elbows.
mistakes: Using momentum or lifting the chest off the pad.
Turning it into a row by driving the elbows back and in.
Going too heavy and shortening the range.
Shrugging the traps instead of using the rear delts.

steps: Grip the bar wider than shoulder-width with an overhand grip and sit down.
Anchor your thighs under the pads and plant your feet flat.
Sit tall, lean back slightly, and lift your chest toward the bar.
Pull the bar down to your upper chest by driving your elbows down and back.
Squeeze your lats at the bottom with the bar near your collarbone.
Return the bar under control until your arms are fully extended and repeat.
cues: Drive the elbows down toward your hips to lead with the lats.
Depress and retract the shoulder blades before pulling.
Exhale as you pull down, inhale as you return.
Get a full stretch at the top on each rep.
mistakes: Leaning back excessively and turning it into a row.
Pulling the bar behind the neck.
Using momentum and yanking the bar down.
Not fully extending the arms at the top.

steps: Attach a close neutral-grip handle and sit with your thighs under the pads.
Grip the handles with palms facing each other and sit tall.
Lean back slightly and lift your chest as you set your shoulder blades down.
Pull the handle down to your upper chest, driving your elbows down and back.
Squeeze your lats at the bottom with a strong contraction.
Return under control until your arms are fully extended and repeat.
cues: The neutral grip emphasizes the lats through a long range.
Drive the elbows toward the floor and back pockets.
Exhale as you pull, inhale as you return.
Stretch fully at the top each rep.
mistakes: Rowing with the torso instead of pulling with the lats.
Using momentum to jerk the weight.
Cutting the stretch short at the top.
Shrugging the shoulders up during the pull.

steps: Attach a single handle to a high pulley and kneel on the leg on the same side as the working arm.
Grip the handle overhead and brace your core with a tall spine.
Reach up to feel a full stretch through the lat.
Pull the handle down toward your hip, driving your elbow down and back.
Squeeze the lat hard at the bottom of the pull.
Return under control to full stretch and repeat, then switch sides.
cues: The single-arm setup lets you focus on one lat at a time.
Drive the elbow toward the hip to lead with the lat.
Exhale as you pull, inhale as you reach up.
Keep the torso stable and avoid twisting.
mistakes: Twisting the torso to help pull the weight.
Cutting the overhead stretch short.
Using momentum instead of a controlled pull.
Letting the shoulder shrug up at the top.

steps: Set an incline bench and lie chest-down with your torso supported against the pad.
Let a dumbbell hang in each hand or grip the machine handles with arms extended.
Set your shoulder blades and keep your head neutral.
Row the weight toward your lower ribs by driving your elbows back.
Squeeze your mid-back and lats at the top.
Lower under control until your arms are extended and repeat.
cues: Chest support removes momentum so the back does the work.
Drive the elbows back and squeeze the shoulder blades.
Exhale as you row, inhale as you lower.
Keep a full range with a stretch at the bottom.
mistakes: Lifting the chest off the pad to cheat.
Shrugging the traps instead of rowing with the back.
Using too heavy a weight and shortening the range.
Jerking the weight up with momentum.

steps: Sit at the cable row station and place your feet on the platform with knees slightly bent.
Grab the handle and sit tall with a neutral spine and slight forward lean to reach.
Set your shoulder blades and brace your core.
Pull the handle toward your lower stomach, driving your elbows straight back.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the pull.
Return under control, allowing a slight stretch forward, and repeat.
cues: Drive the elbows straight back and squeeze the mid-back.
Keep your torso mostly upright and avoid heavy swinging.
Exhale as you pull, inhale as you return.
Allow a controlled stretch at the front each rep.
mistakes: Rocking the torso back and forth for momentum.
Rounding the lower back during the stretch.
Shrugging the shoulders up at the top.
Using a partial range and not extending forward.

steps: Load one end of a barbell in a landmine or corner and stand perpendicular to it.
Stagger your stance and hinge at the hips with a flat back, gripping the sleeve end overhand.
Let the bar hang so your working arm is fully extended and your lat is stretched.
Row the bar up and back by driving your elbow high and toward your hip.
Squeeze the lat and upper back at the top.
Lower under control to a full stretch and repeat, then switch sides.
cues: Drive the elbow up and back to hit the lat and upper back.
Keep a flat back and braced core throughout.
Exhale as you row, inhale as you lower.
Feel a deep stretch at the bottom of each rep.
mistakes: Rounding the lower back at the hinge.
Using body english to heave the weight up.
Cutting the stretch short at the bottom.
Twisting the torso instead of rowing with the arm.

steps: Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with an overhand grip.
Hang with arms fully extended and shoulder blades set down and back.
Brace your core and keep a slight hollow-body position.
Pull yourself up by driving your elbows down until your chin clears the bar.
Squeeze your lats at the top.
Lower under control to a full dead hang and repeat.
cues: Drive the elbows down toward your hips to lead with the lats.
Keep the core tight to avoid swinging.
Exhale as you pull up, inhale as you lower.
Control the descent for a full stretch.
mistakes: Using kipping or swinging to complete reps.
Cutting the range short and not reaching full extension.
Shrugging the shoulders up at the bottom.
Failing to clear the chin over the bar.

steps: Hold a dumbbell in each hand and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is near parallel to the floor.
Keep a flat back, soft knees, and let the dumbbells hang beneath your shoulders.
Brace your core and set your shoulder blades.
Row the dumbbells toward your lower ribs by driving your elbows back.
Squeeze your lats and mid-back at the top.
Lower under control until your arms are extended and repeat.
cues: Drive the elbows back and up toward the hips.
Keep the back flat and core braced the whole set.
Exhale as you row, inhale as you lower.
Allow a full stretch at the bottom.
mistakes: Rounding the lower back during the hinge.
Standing too upright and losing back tension.
Using momentum to jerk the weights up.
Shrugging the traps instead of rowing with the back.

steps: Set a cable pulley high with a rope or straight bar, or use a single dumbbell on a bench.
Position yourself so your arms can reach overhead with the lats stretched.
Keep a slight fixed bend in your elbows and brace your core.
Pull the handle or dumbbell in an arc down toward your hips, keeping arms mostly straight.
Squeeze your lats hard as the weight reaches your thighs.
Return under control to the overhead stretch and repeat.
cues: Keep the elbows fixed so the lats drive the motion.
Emphasize the deep overhead stretch on the lats.
Exhale as you pull down, inhale as you return.
Keep the rib cage down to avoid arching.
mistakes: Bending the elbows and turning it into a pulldown.
Overarching the lower back at the top.
Using momentum instead of a controlled arc.
Shortening the range and skipping the stretch.

steps: Grip the bar shoulder-width with an underhand supinated grip.
Hang with arms fully extended and shoulder blades set down and back.
Brace your core and keep a slight hollow-body position.
Pull yourself up by driving your elbows down until your chin clears the bar.
Squeeze your lats and biceps at the top.
Lower under control to a full dead hang and repeat.
cues: The underhand grip adds biceps involvement alongside the lats.
Drive the elbows down and slightly forward.
Exhale as you pull up, inhale as you lower.
Control the descent for a full stretch.
mistakes: Kipping or swinging to complete reps.
Not reaching full extension at the bottom.
Failing to clear the chin over the bar.
Shrugging the shoulders up at the bottom.

steps: Load a barbell and hinge at the hips until your torso is near parallel to the floor with a flat back.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with an overhand grip.
Let the bar hang at arms length and brace your core hard.
Row the bar toward your lower ribs or belly by driving your elbows back.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top.
Lower under control to full extension and repeat, resetting each rep for a Pendlay style.
cues: Keep the back flat and core braced to protect the spine.
Drive the elbows back and squeeze the mid-back.
Exhale as you row, inhale as you lower.
Keep the bar path close to your body.
mistakes: Rounding the lower back under load.
Standing up too much and using body momentum.
Shrugging the traps instead of rowing with the back.
Bouncing the bar off the floor without control.

steps: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and stand facing the machine.
Grip the bar with arms extended overhead and step back slightly for tension.
Hinge forward a touch, keep a flat back, and brace your core.
Keep your arms nearly straight and push the bar down in an arc to your thighs.
Squeeze your lats hard at the bottom.
Return under control to the overhead stretch and repeat.
cues: Keep the elbows nearly locked so the lats do the work.
Feel the stretch at the top and the squeeze at the bottom.
Exhale as you push down, inhale as you return.
Move only at the shoulder joint.
mistakes: Bending the elbows and turning it into a pushdown.
Using momentum or body swing.
Rounding the lower back on the hinge.
Shortening the range and skipping the stretch.

steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and the barbell over your mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips and bend your knees to grip the bar just outside your legs.
Set a flat back, brace your core, and pull the slack out of the bar.
Drive through your heels and extend your hips and knees together to lift the bar.
Stand tall at the top with hips fully extended and shoulders back.
Lower the bar under control by pushing your hips back and bending the knees, then repeat.
cues: Keep the bar close to your body throughout the lift.
Brace your core hard before every rep.
Push the floor away rather than yanking the bar up.
Keep a neutral spine and avoid rounding.
mistakes: Rounding the lower back under load.
Letting the hips shoot up first and turning it into a stiff-leg pull.
Letting the bar drift away from the shins.
Hyperextending or leaning back at lockout.

steps: Set up in a high plank position gripping a dumbbell in each hand under your shoulders.
Widen your feet for stability and brace your core and glutes.
Keep your hips level and square to the floor.
Row one dumbbell up toward your hip by driving the elbow back.
Lower the dumbbell under control and place it down.
Repeat on the other side, alternating while minimizing hip rotation.
cues: Keep the hips still and square, resisting rotation.
Brace the core hard like a moving plank.
Exhale as you row, inhale as you lower.
Widen the stance for a stable base.
mistakes: Letting the hips twist or rock with each row.
Placing the feet too narrow and losing balance.
Sagging the hips out of plank alignment.
Using momentum to yank the weight up.

steps: Set a bench to a high incline of about 60 degrees and lie chest-down against the pad.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand hanging straight down with palms facing each other.
Set your shoulder blades and keep your head neutral.
Row and shrug the dumbbells up toward your upper chest, driving the elbows back and up.
Squeeze the upper back and traps at the top.
Lower under control to a full stretch and repeat.
cues: Combine a row and a shrug to hit the upper back and traps.
Drive the elbows up high toward the ceiling.
Exhale as you pull, inhale as you lower.
Keep the chest pinned to the pad.
mistakes: Lifting the chest off the pad to cheat.
Using momentum to swing the weights up.
Cutting the stretch short at the bottom.
Going too heavy and losing the upper-back focus.

steps: Set two low pulleys and grab the opposite handle in each hand so the cables cross in front of you.
Stand tall with arms extended down and across your body, cables under tension.
Keep your arms nearly straight and brace your core.
Sweep both arms out and up overhead in a wide arc until they nearly touch above you.
Rotate the pinkies up as you reach the top for peak delt contraction.
Lower slowly along the same arc and repeat.
cues: Use light weight and keep the arms long through a big range.
Sweep in a wide arc all the way overhead.
Exhale as you raise, inhale as you lower.
Keep the movement smooth and controlled.
mistakes: Going too heavy and using momentum.
Bending the elbows to cheat the weight up.
Shrugging the traps instead of using the delts.
Rushing the reps and losing the arc.

steps: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip.
Set your feet hip-width apart and brace your core.
Keep your arms straight and shoulders relaxed to start.
Elevate your shoulders straight up toward your ears using your traps.
Pause and squeeze hard at the top.
Lower under control to a full stretch and repeat.
cues: Shrug straight up and down, not in circles.
Pause and squeeze the traps at the top.
Exhale as you shrug up, inhale as you lower.
Get a full stretch at the bottom each rep.
mistakes: Rolling the shoulders instead of shrugging straight up.
Using momentum to bounce the weight.
Bending the elbows and turning it into a row.
Cutting the range short at the top or bottom.

steps: Attach a rope to a pulley set at about face height and step back to create tension.
Grab both rope ends with a neutral grip and arms extended in front of you.
Stand tall with a slight lean back and braced core.
Pull the rope toward your face, flaring your elbows out and high.
Separate the rope ends and externally rotate so your hands finish beside your ears.
Return under control to the extended start and repeat.
cues: Aim for your forehead and keep the elbows high.
Externally rotate so knuckles point back at the finish.
Exhale as you pull, inhale as you return.
Squeeze the rear delts and mid-back.
mistakes: Pulling too low toward the chest.
Using too much weight and leaning back to yank.
Skipping the external rotation.
Shrugging the traps instead of using rear delts.

steps: Lie face-up on a flat or low-incline bench positioned in front of a low cable pulley with a rope.
Reach back and grab both rope ends with a neutral grip, arms extended toward the pulley.
Brace your core and set your shoulders down.
Pull the rope toward your forehead, flaring your elbows out and wide.
Separate the rope ends and externally rotate at the top, squeezing the rear delts.
Return under control to full extension and repeat.
cues: Lying down removes momentum and cheating.
Pull toward the forehead with elbows high and wide.
Exhale as you pull, inhale as you return.
Focus on the rear delts through external rotation.
mistakes: Pulling too low toward the chest.
Using body movement instead of the rear delts.
Skipping the external rotation at the top.
Loading too heavy and shortening the range.

steps: Attach a wide bar to a cable row or set up with a barbell and take a wider than shoulder-width overhand grip.
Set a flat back with a slight lean and brace your core.
Set your shoulder blades and keep your chest up.
Row the bar toward your upper stomach or lower chest, driving your elbows out and back.
Squeeze your mid-back and rear delts at the top.
Return under control to a stretch and repeat.
cues: The wide grip and flared elbows bias the upper back and rear delts.
Drive the elbows out and back, squeezing the shoulder blades.
Exhale as you row, inhale as you return.
Keep the torso stable and avoid swinging.
mistakes: Using momentum to heave the weight.
Rounding the lower back.
Shrugging the traps up during the pull.
Cutting the range short and skipping the stretch.

steps: Set a single pulley to about the lowest position and attach a handle.
Grab the handle and face away from the machine so the cable runs behind you.
Step forward until your arm is extended behind your body with the biceps stretched.
Keeping your upper arm still, curl the handle up toward your shoulder.
Squeeze the biceps hard at the top of the curl.
Lower under control back to the stretched position and repeat, then switch arms.
cues: Facing away puts the biceps under a deep stretch for growth.
Keep the upper arm fixed and only move at the elbow.
Exhale as you curl, inhale as you lower.
Emphasize the stretched bottom position.
mistakes: Letting the elbow drift forward during the curl.
Using body momentum to swing the weight.
Cutting the stretch short at the bottom.
Going too heavy and losing strict form.

steps: Set a preacher bench so the pad sits under your armpits at roughly a 45 degree angle.
Rest the back of your upper arms flat on the pad and grip the bar or dumbbell.
Start with your arms nearly extended, feeling a stretch in the biceps.
Curl the weight up toward your shoulders by contracting the biceps.
Squeeze at the top without letting the elbows lift off the pad.
Lower under control to near full extension and repeat.
cues: Keep the upper arms flat on the pad throughout.
Emphasize the stretch at the bottom of each rep.
Exhale as you curl, inhale as you lower.
Avoid fully locking or slamming into the bottom.
mistakes: Bouncing out of the bottom stretched position.
Lifting the elbows off the pad to cheat.
Using momentum and swinging the torso.
Cutting the range short at the top or bottom.

steps: Set a bench to about a 45 to 60 degree incline and sit back with a dumbbell in each hand.
Let your arms hang straight down behind your torso with palms facing forward.
Keep your upper arms still and your shoulders back against the pad.
Curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders by contracting the biceps.
Squeeze at the top, then lower under control.
Return to the fully stretched hanging position and repeat.
cues: The incline puts the biceps in a lengthened, stretched position.
Keep the elbows back and fixed by your sides.
Exhale as you curl, inhale as you lower.
Emphasize the deep stretch at the bottom.
mistakes: Letting the elbows swing forward during the curl.
Using momentum to heave the weights.
Cutting the stretch short at the bottom.
Shrugging the shoulders forward off the pad.

steps: Attach a straight bar or EZ handle to a low pulley and stand facing the machine.
Grip the bar with an underhand grip and step back for tension, arms extended down.
Pin your elbows to your sides and brace your core.
Curl the bar up toward your shoulders by contracting the biceps.
Squeeze hard at the top of the curl.
Lower under control to full extension and repeat.
cues: The cable keeps constant tension throughout the range.
Keep the elbows pinned and only move at the forearm.
Exhale as you curl, inhale as you lower.
Control the negative and avoid swinging.
mistakes: Letting the elbows drift forward or flare out.
Using body momentum to swing the weight.
Cutting the range short at the bottom.
Leaning back to help lift heavier loads.

steps: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, palms facing your thighs.
Pin your elbows to your sides and brace your core.
Keep your wrists neutral and thumbs pointing up.
Curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders, maintaining the neutral grip.
Squeeze at the top, keeping the palms facing inward.
Lower under control to full extension and repeat.
cues: The neutral grip targets the brachialis and forearms.
Keep the elbows pinned and wrists neutral.
Exhale as you curl, inhale as you lower.
Avoid swinging and control the descent.
mistakes: Swinging the torso to heave the weights.
Letting the elbows drift forward.
Rotating the wrists instead of keeping them neutral.
Using momentum and cutting the range short.

steps: Load an EZ-bar or straight barbell and grip it underhand at about shoulder-width.
Stand tall with elbows pinned to your sides and core braced.
Let the bar hang at arms length in front of your thighs.
Curl the bar up toward your shoulders by contracting the biceps.
Squeeze at the top without swinging your torso.
Lower under control to full extension and repeat.
cues: Keep the elbows fixed at your sides throughout.
The angled EZ-bar grip is easier on the wrists.
Exhale as you curl, inhale as you lower.
Control the negative and avoid body swing.
mistakes: Swinging the torso and using momentum.
Letting the elbows drift forward.
Cutting the range short at the bottom.
Leaning back to heave heavier weight.

steps: Sit on a bench with your legs spread and lean forward slightly, holding a dumbbell in one hand.
Brace the back of your working upper arm against the inside of your thigh.
Let the dumbbell hang with your arm fully extended.
Curl the dumbbell up toward your shoulder by contracting the biceps.
Squeeze hard at the top of the curl.
Lower under control to full extension and repeat, then switch arms.
cues: Bracing the arm on the thigh fully isolates the biceps.
Squeeze the peak contraction at the top.
Exhale as you curl, inhale as you lower.
Control the negative for full tension.
mistakes: Using momentum to swing the dumbbell up.
Lifting the elbow off the thigh.
Cutting the range short at the bottom.
Rushing the reps and losing control.

steps: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms hanging and palms facing forward.
Keep your elbows pinned close to your sides and shoulders back.
Curl the dumbbells upward by flexing at the elbow, squeezing the biceps.
Stop just short of the shoulders to keep tension on the muscle.
Lower under control until the arms are fully extended.
Repeat for the desired reps without swinging.
cues: Lower over two to three seconds for controlled eccentric tension.
Keep the upper arm still and let only the forearm move.
Squeeze the biceps hard at the top of each rep.
mistakes: Swinging the torso to heave the weight up.
Letting the elbows drift forward and turning it into a front raise.
Cutting the range short by not fully extending at the bottom.

steps: Set an incline bench to about 45 degrees and lie chest-down against the pad.
Let your arms hang straight down from the shoulders holding dumbbells or an EZ-bar.
Keep your chest pressed into the bench throughout the set.
Curl the weight up by contracting the biceps while keeping the upper arms vertical.
Squeeze hard at the top where tension peaks.
Lower slowly until the arms are fully straight and repeat.
cues: Keep the upper arms perpendicular to the floor the whole rep.
Emphasize a slow eccentric to maximize the stretch.
Think about squeezing the biceps rather than lifting the weight.
mistakes: Lifting the chest off the pad to cheat the weight up.
Swinging the elbows back to shorten the range.
Using too heavy a load and losing strict form.

steps: Stand tall and hold a single dumbbell vertically with both hands cupped under the top plate.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your ribs.
Curl the dumbbell up toward your chin by flexing the biceps.
Pause and squeeze at the top with wrists neutral.
Lower slowly until the arms are fully extended.
Repeat while keeping the torso upright.
cues: Keep wrists straight so the biceps do the work.
Pin the elbows to your sides for strict movement.
Control the lowering phase for constant tension.
mistakes: Letting the wrists bend back and shifting load to the forearms.
Swinging the body to move the weight.
Flaring the elbows outward during the curl.

steps: Attach a straight or EZ bar to a low pulley and face away from the machine.
Grip the bar and raise it overhead with elbows bent behind your head.
Stagger your stance and brace your core for stability.
Extend your elbows to press the bar up until the arms are straight overhead.
Squeeze the triceps at full lockout.
Lower the bar back behind the head under control and repeat.
cues: Keep the upper arms fixed and pointed toward the ceiling.
Feel the long head of the triceps stretch at the bottom.
Exhale as you extend and lock out fully.
mistakes: Letting the elbows flare wide and drop forward.
Arching the lower back to push the weight.
Shortening the range by not lowering fully behind the head.

steps: Set a rope on a low pulley or grab a single dumbbell overhead with both hands.
Raise your arms overhead with elbows bent and hands behind your head.
Keep your upper arms close to your ears and core braced.
Extend the elbows to drive the rope or dumbbell up until straight.
Spread the rope apart or squeeze the triceps at the top.
Lower under control into a deep stretch and repeat.
cues: Keep the elbows tucked and pointing forward, not flaring out.
Emphasize the stretch on the long head at the bottom.
Move only at the elbow joint.
mistakes: Flaring the elbows out to the sides.
Overarching the lower back under load.
Using momentum instead of a controlled tempo.

steps: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley and grip it with palms facing down.
Stand tall with a slight forward lean and pin your elbows to your sides.
Start with the forearms roughly parallel to the floor.
Press the bar down by extending the elbows until the arms are straight.
Squeeze the triceps hard at the bottom.
Return the bar slowly until the forearms are parallel again and repeat.
cues: Keep the elbows locked at your sides throughout.
Only the forearms should move.
Fully lock out and squeeze at the bottom.
mistakes: Letting the elbows drift forward and away from the body.
Leaning heavily to push with bodyweight.
Using a partial range and not locking out.

steps: Set a cable to a low pulley and attach a single handle.
Hinge forward at the hips with a flat back and grip the handle.
Raise your upper arm so it is parallel to the floor and pinned to your side.
Extend the elbow back until the arm is fully straight.
Squeeze the triceps at full extension.
Return slowly to the bent-elbow start and repeat before switching arms.
cues: Keep the upper arm still and parallel to the torso.
Squeeze and hold briefly at lockout for peak contraction.
Control the return rather than letting the cable pull you.
mistakes: Dropping the upper arm during the movement.
Swinging the shoulder to fling the weight back.
Rounding the back while hinging.

steps: Lie on a flat bench holding an EZ-bar with a shoulder-width grip.
Press the bar up so the arms are extended over your chest.
Angle the arms slightly back so the elbows point toward your head.
Bend the elbows to lower the bar toward your forehead or just behind it.
Keep the upper arms still as you extend back to the start.
Lock out the triceps at the top and repeat.
cues: Keep the elbows tucked and pointing forward, not flaring.
Lower under control for a strong triceps stretch.
Angle the arms back slightly to keep tension at lockout.
mistakes: Flaring the elbows outward as you lower.
Moving the upper arms and turning it into a press.
Bouncing the bar off the forehead.

steps: Lie on a flat bench and grip the bar just inside shoulder width.
Set your shoulder blades back and down and plant your feet.
Unrack the bar and hold it over your chest with straight arms.
Lower the bar to your lower chest while keeping the elbows tucked.
Press the bar back up by driving through the triceps to lockout.
Repeat while keeping the wrists stacked over the elbows.
cues: Keep elbows tucked at roughly 45 degrees to load the triceps.
Grip about shoulder width, not excessively narrow, to protect the wrists.
Drive up explosively and lock out the elbows.
mistakes: Gripping too narrow and straining the wrists.
Flaring the elbows wide and shifting load to the chest.
Bouncing the bar off the chest.

steps: Attach a rope to a high pulley and grip an end in each hand with a neutral grip.
Stand tall with a slight forward lean and pin your elbows to your sides.
Start with the forearms about parallel to the floor.
Press down and extend the elbows while spreading the rope apart at the bottom.
Squeeze the triceps hard at full extension.
Return slowly to the start and repeat.
cues: Split the rope ends apart at the bottom for a stronger contraction.
Keep elbows glued to your sides.
Control the eccentric back to the start.
mistakes: Letting the elbows travel forward off the ribs.
Using bodyweight to bob the weight down.
Failing to spread the rope and lock out fully.

steps: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley and grip it with palms facing up.
Stand tall and pin your elbows firmly to your sides.
Begin with the forearms roughly parallel to the floor.
Extend the elbows to press the bar down until the arms are straight.
Squeeze the triceps at the bottom, biasing the medial head.
Return the bar slowly to the start and repeat.
cues: Keep the wrists neutral and elbows tight to the body.
Only the forearms move through the rep.
Full lockout with a firm squeeze at the bottom.
mistakes: Letting the elbows drift forward.
Leaning in to push with the torso.
Using a partial range and skipping lockout.

steps: Hold a dumbbell in one hand and hinge forward with a flat back.
Brace your free hand on a bench for support.
Raise your upper arm until it is parallel to the floor and pinned to your side.
Extend the elbow back until the arm is fully straight.
Squeeze the triceps at full extension.
Lower slowly to the bent-elbow start and repeat before switching sides.
cues: Keep the upper arm locked parallel to the torso.
Squeeze and pause at lockout for the peak contraction.
Control the lowering phase rather than dropping the weight.
mistakes: Dropping the elbow during the rep.
Swinging the arm to generate momentum.
Rounding the back while hinged over.

steps: Sit and rest your forearms on your thighs or a bench with palms facing up.
Let your wrists and the barbell or dumbbells hang off the edge.
Allow the weight to roll down toward your fingertips for a full stretch.
Curl the weight up by flexing your wrists as high as possible.
Squeeze the forearm flexors at the top.
Lower under control back to the stretched position and repeat.
cues: Keep the forearms flat and still, moving only the wrists.
Let the bar roll to the fingers for a full range.
Use a slow, controlled tempo throughout.
mistakes: Lifting the forearms off the support to cheat.
Using too much weight and shortening the range.
Rushing reps and losing the stretch at the bottom.

steps: Sit and rest your forearms on your thighs or a bench with palms facing down.
Let your wrists and the weight hang off the edge.
Lower the weight by dropping the knuckles toward the floor for a stretch.
Extend the wrists upward by lifting the knuckles as high as possible.
Squeeze the forearm extensors at the top.
Lower slowly back to the start and repeat.
cues: Keep the forearms pinned and motionless.
Move only at the wrist joint.
Use lighter weight and a controlled tempo.
mistakes: Raising the forearms to assist the lift.
Overloading and cutting the range short.
Jerking the weight instead of controlling it.

steps: Stand tall holding a barbell behind your glutes with palms facing back.
Keep your arms straight and elbows near your sides.
Let the bar roll down toward your fingertips for a stretch.
Curl the wrists up, flexing the forearms behind your back.
Squeeze at the top of the contraction.
Lower under control to the stretched position and repeat.
cues: Keep the arms straight and stationary throughout.
Isolate the movement to the wrists only.
Control the roll to the fingertips for full range.
mistakes: Bending the elbows to help lift the bar.
Using excessive weight and losing range.
Swinging the bar with body movement.

steps: Stand tall holding a barbell or EZ-bar with an overhand, palms-down grip.
Keep your elbows pinned to your sides and shoulders back.
Curl the bar up by flexing at the elbow while keeping wrists straight.
Stop just short of the shoulders to maintain tension.
Squeeze the brachialis and forearms at the top.
Lower under control until the arms are fully extended and repeat.
cues: Keep the wrists firm and neutral to target the brachialis and brachioradialis.
Pin the elbows to your sides through the rep.
Use a slow eccentric for maximal tension.
mistakes: Letting the wrists curl upward and cheat the load.
Swinging the body to move heavier weight.
Allowing the elbows to drift forward.

steps: Stand between two heavy dumbbells or handles and hinge to grip them.
Brace your core, set your shoulders back and down, and stand up tall.
Keep your chest up, arms straight, and grip tight.
Walk forward with short, controlled steps for the prescribed distance or time.
Keep the weights from swinging and stay upright the whole way.
Set the weights down under control with a hip hinge.
cues: Stay tall with ribs down and shoulders packed.
Grip the handles hard the entire set.
Breathe steadily while bracing the core.
mistakes: Leaning or shrugging excessively while walking.
Letting the weights swing and pull you off balance.
Rounding the back when picking up or setting down.

steps: Position your shoulders under the pads and back flat against the machine.
Place your feet shoulder-width, roughly centered on the platform.
Release the safeties and brace your core.
Lower by bending the knees until your thighs pass parallel.
Drive through your whole foot to press back up.
Stop just short of locking the knees and repeat.
cues: Keep your back and hips flat against the pad throughout.
Push the knees out in line with your toes.
Control the descent and drive powerfully out of the bottom.
mistakes: Letting the lower back round off the pad at the bottom.
Letting the knees cave inward.
Cutting depth short and using a partial range.

steps: Set your shoulders under the pads and back against the backrest.
Place your feet shoulder-width on the platform and brace your core.
Release the safeties to take the load.
Descend by bending the knees and hips along the arced path until thighs pass parallel.
Drive through your feet to press back up.
Stop just short of lockout and repeat.
cues: Keep your back flush against the pad the whole set.
Track the knees out over the toes.
Emphasize a controlled eccentric and deep stretch.
mistakes: Rounding the lower back at the bottom.
Letting the knees collapse inward.
Bouncing out of the bottom instead of controlling it.

steps: Set the bar across your upper traps and grip it firmly with your hands.
Unrack the bar and step back into a shoulder-width stance with toes slightly out.
Brace your core and take a big breath.
Sit down and back by bending the hips and knees until thighs reach parallel or below.
Drive through your whole foot to stand back up.
Lock out the hips at the top and repeat.
cues: Brace hard and keep the chest up throughout.
Push the knees out in line with the toes.
Drive the floor away and keep the bar over midfoot.
mistakes: Letting the knees cave inward on the way up.
Rounding the lower back at the bottom.
Rising with the hips first and tipping the torso forward.

steps: Set the bar across your upper traps and unlock it from the hooks.
Place your feet slightly forward of the bar in a shoulder-width stance.
Brace your core and take a breath.
Lower by bending the hips and knees until your thighs reach parallel.
Drive through your feet to press back up along the fixed bar path.
Lock out at the top and repeat.
cues: Position the feet slightly forward so the knees stay comfortable.
Keep the core braced and chest tall.
Push the knees out over the toes.
mistakes: Placing the feet directly under the bar and stressing the knees.
Rounding the lower back at depth.
Cutting depth short above parallel.

steps: Stand a stride length in front of a bench and place one foot behind you on it.
Hold dumbbells at your sides or a single weight at your chest.
Keep your torso slightly forward and your front shin fairly vertical.
Lower straight down until your front thigh reaches about parallel.
Drive through the front heel to return to standing.
Complete all reps, then switch legs.
cues: Keep most of the weight on the front leg.
Lower under control and keep the front knee tracking over the toes.
Stay tall through the spine while leaning slightly forward at the hip.
mistakes: Placing the front foot too close and overloading the knee.
Pushing off the rear foot instead of the front leg.
Letting the front knee cave inward.

steps: Rack the bar across the front of your shoulders and raise your elbows high.
Grip the bar with fingers or crossed arms to hold it in place.
Step back into a shoulder-width stance with toes slightly out.
Brace your core and descend by bending the hips and knees until thighs reach parallel or below.
Drive through your feet and keep the torso upright to stand back up.
Lock out the hips at the top and repeat.
cues: Keep the elbows high to prevent the bar rolling forward.
Maintain an upright torso throughout.
Brace hard and push the knees out over the toes.
mistakes: Letting the elbows drop and the chest cave forward.
Rounding the upper back under the load.
Rising with the hips and losing the upright posture.

steps: Sit in the machine with your back against the pad and knees at the pivot point.
Set the ankle pad just above your feet against the shins.
Grip the handles and keep your hips down in the seat.
Extend your knees to lift the pad until your legs are nearly straight.
Squeeze the quads hard at the top.
Lower under control to the start and repeat.
cues: Squeeze the quads and pause briefly at full extension.
Lower slowly to keep tension on the muscle.
Keep your hips and back pinned to the seat.
mistakes: Using momentum to swing the pad up.
Lifting the hips off the seat to gain leverage.
Dropping the weight quickly on the way down.

steps: Kneel upright on a pad with your knees hip-width and feet behind you.
Keep your hips extended and your torso in a straight line with your thighs.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes.
Lean back slowly by bending only at the knees, keeping the body rigid.
Descend as far as your quads allow while maintaining control.
Contract the quads to pull yourself back upright and repeat.
cues: Keep the hips extended so you hinge only at the knees.
Move slowly to control the deep quad stretch.
Keep the torso and thighs in one straight line.
mistakes: Bending at the hips instead of the knees.
Descending too fast and losing control.
Going deeper than you can control on the way back up.

steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width, holding dumbbells at your sides or bodyweight only.
Step forward into a long stride and brace your core.
Lower straight down until both knees reach about 90 degrees.
Drive through the front heel to bring the rear foot forward.
Step directly into the next lunge with the other leg.
Continue alternating for the prescribed distance or reps.
cues: Keep the torso upright and core braced.
Push through the front heel to power each step.
Take a long enough stride to keep the front knee over the ankle.
mistakes: Taking short steps that push the front knee past the toes.
Letting the front knee cave inward.
Leaning the torso too far forward.

steps: Hold a dumbbell or kettlebell vertically against your chest with both hands.
Stand with feet slightly wider than shoulder-width and toes turned out.
Brace your core and keep your chest tall and elbows down.
Sit down between your legs until your thighs reach at least parallel.
Drive through your whole foot to stand back up.
Lock out the hips at the top and repeat.
cues: Keep the weight close to your chest and elbows inside your knees.
Push the knees out in line with the toes.
Sit straight down while keeping the chest up.
mistakes: Letting the chest collapse and the back round.
Allowing the knees to cave inward.
Rising onto the toes instead of driving through the whole foot.

steps: Sit in the machine with your back and hips flat against the pad.
Place your feet shoulder-width on the platform, roughly centered.
Release the safeties and brace your core.
Bend your knees to lower the platform toward your chest until the thighs approach the torso.
Drive through your whole foot to press the platform away.
Stop just short of locking the knees and repeat.
cues: Keep your lower back and hips pinned to the pad.
Push the knees out in line with the toes.
Control the negative and avoid slamming the stack.
mistakes: Letting the hips round off the pad at the bottom.
Locking the knees hard at the top.
Placing feet too low and letting the heels lift.

steps: Hold a pair of dumbbells at your shoulders and set a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Brace your core and, if using a Bosu, balance evenly on the dome.
Descend into a squat until your thighs reach about parallel.
Drive up through your feet to stand explosively.
Use that momentum to press the dumbbells overhead to lockout.
Lower the weights to your shoulders and repeat the sequence.
cues: Time the press to flow out of the top of the squat.
Keep the core tight for balance and spinal safety.
Press the weights straight overhead and lock out.
mistakes: Pressing before finishing the squat, breaking the sequence.
Overarching the lower back during the overhead press.
Using an unstable surface with loads too heavy to control.

steps: Stand holding a barbell at your thighs with a shoulder-width grip.
Set your feet hip-width, soften the knees, and brace your core.
Push your hips back and lower the bar along your legs.
Keep your back flat and lower until you feel a strong hamstring stretch.
Drive your hips forward to stand back up, keeping the bar close.
Squeeze your glutes at the top and repeat.
cues: Lead with the hips traveling backward, not by bending the knees.
Keep the bar dragging close to your legs the whole time.
Feel the stretch in the hamstrings, not the lower back.
mistakes: Rounding the lower back as you lower.
Turning it into a squat by bending the knees too much.
Letting the bar drift away from the body.

steps: Sit in the machine with your back against the pad and knees at the pivot.
Set the ankle pad against your lower calves and the thigh pad snug over your legs.
Grip the handles and keep your hips pinned in the seat.
Curl your heels down and under by flexing the knees as far as possible.
Squeeze the hamstrings hard at the bottom.
Return under control to the start and repeat.
cues: Squeeze and pause at peak contraction.
Control the eccentric back to full extension.
Keep the hips down and thighs locked under the pad.
mistakes: Lifting the hips to generate leverage.
Using momentum to fling the pad down.
Cutting the range short at the top or bottom.

steps: Lie face-down on the machine with your knees just off the edge of the pad.
Set the ankle pad against the back of your lower calves.
Grip the handles and keep your hips pressed into the bench.
Curl your heels toward your glutes by flexing the knees.
Squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the curl.
Lower under control to full extension and repeat.
cues: Keep the hips down and avoid arching to cheat the weight.
Squeeze the hamstrings at the top.
Lower slowly for constant tension.
mistakes: Lifting the hips off the pad to swing the weight up.
Using momentum instead of controlled reps.
Not fully extending the knees between reps.

steps: Stand holding a barbell at your thighs with a shoulder-width grip.
Set your feet hip-width and keep your knees nearly straight but not locked.
Brace your core and set a flat back.
Hinge at the hips and lower the bar down your legs until you feel a deep hamstring stretch.
Drive your hips forward to stand back up, keeping the bar close.
Squeeze the glutes at the top and repeat.
cues: Keep the legs nearly straight to bias the hamstrings.
Maintain a flat back and hinge from the hips.
Keep the bar traveling close to your legs.
mistakes: Rounding the lower back under load.
Bending the knees too much and reducing hamstring stretch.
Letting the bar drift forward away from the body.

steps: Kneel on a pad and anchor your ankles under a secure pad or partner's hands.
Keep your hips extended and your body in a straight line from knees to head.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes.
Lower your torso toward the floor slowly, resisting with your hamstrings.
Descend as far as you can control, then catch yourself with your hands if needed.
Pull yourself back up using the hamstrings and repeat.
cues: Keep the hips fully extended so you hinge at the knees.
Fight the descent to maximize the eccentric.
Keep the body rigid in one straight line.
mistakes: Breaking at the hips instead of staying straight.
Dropping too fast and losing eccentric control.
Not securing the ankles firmly before starting.

steps: Set a barbell across your upper traps as you would for a squat.
Stand with feet hip- to shoulder-width and soften the knees.
Brace your core hard and set a flat back.
Push your hips back and hinge your torso forward toward parallel.
Keep the back flat and feel the hamstrings stretch.
Drive your hips forward to return upright and repeat.
cues: Lead the movement with the hips traveling backward.
Keep the spine neutral and chest proud.
Use a light, controlled load to protect the lower back.
mistakes: Rounding the back as you hinge forward.
Bending mostly at the knees instead of the hips.
Using too much weight and losing spinal position.

steps: Set the pad of a 45-degree back extension just below your hip crease.
Anchor your feet and let your torso hang down over the pad.
Keep a slightly rounded upper back and soft knees to bias the hamstrings.
Brace and raise your torso by extending the hips until your body is straight.
Squeeze the hamstrings and glutes at the top without hyperextending.
Lower under control back to the stretched position and repeat.
cues: Drive the movement from the hips and hamstrings.
Stop at a straight-line body position, do not overextend.
Keep a controlled tempo through the stretch.
mistakes: Hyperextending the lower back at the top.
Using momentum to swing up.
Setting the pad too high above the hips.

steps: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Brace your core and step forward into a long stride.
Lower straight down until both knees reach about 90 degrees.
Drive through the front heel to bring the back foot forward.
Step directly into the next lunge with the opposite leg.
Continue alternating for the prescribed distance or reps.
cues: Keep the torso upright and shoulders back.
Push through the front heel to power each rep.
Take a long stride so the front shin stays near vertical.
mistakes: Short strides that push the knee past the toes.
Letting the front knee cave inward.
Leaning forward and letting the dumbbells pull you off balance.

steps: Set the pad of a 45-degree back extension just below your hip crease.
Anchor your feet and turn your toes out slightly to engage the glutes.
Let your torso hang down and keep a neutral to slightly rounded upper back.
Brace and raise your torso by squeezing the glutes to extend the hips.
Stop at a straight-line body position and squeeze the glutes hard.
Lower under control to the stretch and repeat.
cues: Turn the toes out and think about squeezing the glutes to rise.
Round the upper back slightly to reduce spinal erector involvement.
Stop at neutral, do not hyperextend the lower back.
mistakes: Overextending the spine at the top.
Driving with the lower back instead of the glutes.
Swinging up with momentum.

steps: Set the Smith bar across your upper traps and stand on a small platform under the bar.
Step one foot back into a lunge stance off the platform.
Brace your core and unlock the bar.
Lower straight down until the front thigh drops below parallel using the added deficit.
Drive through the front heel to press back up along the fixed path.
Complete all reps, then switch legs.
cues: Use the deficit to reach a deeper stretch on the front leg.
Keep most of the weight on the front leg.
Track the front knee over the toes.
mistakes: Pushing mainly off the rear foot.
Letting the front knee cave inward.
Standing too far forward and stressing the knee.

steps: Sit in the machine with your back against the pad and outer thighs against the pads.
Set the starting width so your legs begin close together.
Grip the handles and sit tall with your core braced.
Push your legs outward by driving through the glutes.
Squeeze the outer glutes at the widest point.
Return under control to the start and repeat.
cues: Squeeze and pause at the fully abducted position.
Lean slightly forward to bias the upper glutes if desired.
Control the return rather than letting the pads snap back.
mistakes: Using momentum to fling the legs apart.
Letting the weight slam back at the start.
Using such a heavy load that the range is cut short.

steps: Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench and a loaded bar over your hips.
Plant your feet flat, shoulder-width, with shins roughly vertical at the top.
Brace your core and tuck your chin slightly.
Drive through your heels to raise your hips until your torso is parallel to the floor.
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top with a neutral pelvis.
Lower under control and repeat.
cues: Squeeze the glutes hard and pause at full lockout.
Keep the chin tucked and ribs down to avoid arching.
Drive through the heels, not the toes.
mistakes: Overextending the lower back at the top instead of using the glutes.
Placing the feet too far out and losing heel drive.
Cutting the range short and not reaching full hip extension.

steps: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and secure it around one ankle.
Face the machine and hold the frame for support with a slight hip hinge.
Brace your core and keep a small bend in the working knee.
Drive the working leg back and up by squeezing the glute.
Pause and squeeze at full hip extension without arching the back.
Return under control and repeat before switching legs.
cues: Move from the hip and squeeze the glute at the top.
Keep the torso stable and avoid arching the lower back.
Control the return for constant tension.
mistakes: Arching the lower back to swing the leg higher.
Using momentum instead of glute contraction.
Rotating the hips open to cheat the movement.

steps: For donkey kicks and fire hydrants, start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Brace your core and keep a neutral spine throughout.
For the donkey kick, drive one bent leg up and back, squeezing the glute at the top.
For the fire hydrant, lift the bent knee out to the side while keeping the hips level.
For the kettlebell swing, hinge at the hips and hike the bell back, then snap the hips forward to float it to chest height.
Return under control to the start and repeat each movement.
cues: Power the kettlebell swing with an explosive hip snap, not the arms.
Keep the core braced and spine neutral on the floor variations.
Squeeze the glute at the top of each rep.
mistakes: Arching or rounding the lower back to gain range.
Turning the kettlebell swing into a squat or front raise.
Letting the hips rotate or drop out of level.

steps: Position your shoulders under the pads and the balls of your feet on the platform.
Let your heels drop below the platform for a full stretch.
Keep your knees nearly straight and your core braced.
Press up onto the balls of your feet as high as possible.
Squeeze the calves hard at the top.
Lower under control into a deep stretch and repeat.
cues: Pause at the top and squeeze the calves.
Emphasize a deep stretch at the bottom of each rep.
Keep the knees nearly straight to target the gastrocnemius.
mistakes: Bouncing quickly and skipping the stretch.
Using a partial range instead of full up-and-down travel.
Bending the knees excessively during the rep.

steps: Sit in the machine with the balls of your feet on the platform and the pad over your knees.
Let your heels drop below the platform for a full stretch.
Release the safety and keep your feet firmly planted.
Press up onto the balls of your feet as high as possible.
Squeeze the calves at the top of the contraction.
Lower slowly into a deep stretch and repeat.
cues: Pause and squeeze hard at the top.
Let the heels sink for a full stretch at the bottom.
This bent-knee position emphasizes the soleus.
mistakes: Bouncing through reps without a controlled stretch.
Using a short, partial range of motion.
Letting the feet slide off the platform.

steps: Sit in the leg press and place the balls of your feet on the lower edge of the platform.
Release the safeties and keep your legs nearly straight but not locked.
Let the platform push your toes back for a full ankle stretch.
Press the platform away by pushing through the balls of your feet.
Squeeze the calves at full extension.
Return under control into the stretch and repeat.
cues: Keep the knees soft but stable throughout.
Achieve a deep stretch before each press.
Squeeze and pause at full plantarflexion.
mistakes: Locking or bending the knees to move the weight.
Rushing reps and skipping the stretch.
Placing the feet too high and losing range.

steps: Set the Smith bar across your upper traps and place the balls of your feet on a small platform or plate.
Unlock the bar and let your heels drop for a full stretch.
Keep your knees nearly straight and your core braced.
Press up onto the balls of your feet as high as possible.
Squeeze the calves hard at the top.
Lower slowly into the stretch and repeat.
cues: Use the fixed bar path for balance and control.
Pause and squeeze at the top of each rep.
Sink into a deep stretch at the bottom.
mistakes: Bouncing quickly through the reps.
Using a partial range and skipping the stretch.
Standing flat-footed instead of on the balls of the feet.

steps: Attach a rope to a high pulley and kneel facing the machine.
Grip the rope beside your head and hinge slightly forward.
Keep your hips fixed and let the abs bear the load.
Crunch down by flexing your spine and bringing your elbows toward your thighs.
Squeeze the abs hard at the bottom of the crunch.
Return under control to a stretched position and repeat.
cues: Round the spine and crunch with the abs, not the hips.
Squeeze hard at the bottom of each rep.
Keep the hips stationary throughout.
mistakes: Turning it into a hip hinge instead of a spinal crunch.
Pulling with the arms rather than the abs.
Using momentum to yank the weight down.

steps: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip and arms straight.
Brace your core and keep your shoulders active.
Posteriorly tilt your pelvis to initiate the movement.
