Raise your legs by curling your hips up until your thighs pass parallel or higher.
Squeeze the abs at the top of the raise.
Lower your legs under control without swinging and repeat.
cues: Curl the pelvis up rather than just lifting the legs.
Control the descent to avoid swinging.
Keep the shoulders packed and core tight.
mistakes: Using momentum and swinging the body.
Only lifting the legs without tilting the pelvis.
Dropping the legs quickly and losing tension.

steps: Lie on a bench and grip behind your head to anchor your upper body.
Brace your core hard and press your shoulder blades into the bench.
Raise your legs and hips so your body forms a straight line pointing up.
Lower your whole body as one rigid unit, keeping it straight.
Descend as low as you can control while keeping the hips from sagging.
Raise back to the top under control and repeat.
cues: Keep the entire body rigid from shoulders to toes.
Only your shoulders should stay in contact with the bench.
Lower slowly to control the eccentric.
mistakes: Bending at the hips and letting the body pike.
Dropping too fast and losing control.
Arching the lower back away from a straight line.

steps: Set a cable to chest height and attach a single handle.
Stand side-on to the machine and grip the handle with both hands at your chest.
Step out to create tension and set a shoulder-width stance.
Brace your core hard to resist the cable's pull toward the machine.
Press the handle straight out in front of your chest and hold.
Return the handle to your chest under control and repeat before switching sides.
cues: Resist rotation by bracing the core, not by muscling with the arms.
Keep the hips and shoulders square to the front.
Breathe steadily while maintaining the brace.
mistakes: Letting the torso rotate toward the machine.
Standing too close and losing meaningful tension.
Holding your breath instead of bracing and breathing.

steps: Stand beside a single heavy dumbbell or kettlebell and hinge to grip it.
Brace your core, set your shoulders level, and stand up tall.
Keep your torso perfectly upright and resist leaning toward the weight.
Walk forward with controlled steps for the prescribed distance or time.
Fight to keep your shoulders and hips square the entire way.
Set the weight down under control, then repeat on the other side.
cues: Stay tall and resist the urge to lean toward the load.
Keep the shoulders level and core braced against side flexion.
Take steady, controlled steps.
mistakes: Leaning the torso toward the weighted side.
Letting the shoulders or hips tilt.
Rushing the walk and losing an upright posture.

steps: Set a decline bench and secure your legs under the pads.
Hold a weight plate against your chest or behind your head.
Lie back and brace your core to start.
Crunch up by flexing your spine and lifting your shoulders toward your knees.
Squeeze the abs hard at the top of the crunch.
Lower under control back to the start and repeat.
cues: Flex the spine to lift, rather than pulling from the hips.
Squeeze the abs at the top of each rep.
Control the descent for constant tension.
mistakes: Yanking on the neck with the hands.
Using momentum to bounce up.
Only hip-flexing instead of crunching the abs.

steps: Lie on your back with your hands lightly behind your head and knees bent.
Lift your shoulders off the floor and raise your feet.
Brace your core and keep the lower back in contact with the floor.
Bring one elbow toward the opposite knee while extending the other leg.
Rotate through the torso, not just the arms, to touch each side.
Alternate sides in a smooth, controlled pedaling motion.
cues: Rotate from the torso and obliques, not the neck.
Fully extend the straightening leg each rep.
Move slowly and deliberately rather than racing.
mistakes: Pulling on the neck with the hands.
Rushing through reps with no real rotation.
Letting the lower back arch off the floor.

steps: Set your forearms on the floor with elbows directly under your shoulders.
Extend your legs back and rest on your toes.
Form a straight line from head to heels.
Brace your core, squeeze your glutes, and tuck your pelvis slightly.
Hold the position for the prescribed time while breathing steadily.
Lower under control when the set is complete.
cues: Keep a straight line from head to heels.
Brace the abs and squeeze the glutes throughout.
Breathe steadily instead of holding your breath.
mistakes: Letting the hips sag toward the floor.
Piking the hips up too high.
Dropping the head or letting the lower back arch.

steps: Lie face-down on the reverse hyper pad with your hips at the edge and legs hanging down.
Grip the handles firmly and brace your core.
Let your legs hang and swing slightly forward under the platform.
Raise your legs back and up by squeezing the glutes and hamstrings until your body is straight.
Pause and squeeze at the top without overextending.
Lower under control and repeat.
cues: Drive the movement with the glutes and hamstrings.
Stop at a straight-line position, do not hyperextend the spine.
Control the swing rather than using pure momentum.
mistakes: Overextending the lower back at the top.
Using excessive momentum to fling the legs up.
Failing to brace the core against the load.

steps: For the bird dog, start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
Extend one arm forward and the opposite leg back until both are in line with your torso.
Pause and hold, keeping the hips level, then return under control and switch sides.
For the superman, lie face-down with arms extended overhead.
Simultaneously lift your arms, chest, and legs off the floor, squeeze, then lower under control.
cues: Keep the hips square and level during the bird dog.
Move slowly and pause at full extension.
Squeeze the glutes and lower back at the top of the superman.
mistakes: Letting the hips rotate or sag during the bird dog.
Overarching the neck by looking up.
Rushing reps instead of controlling each hold.
